researched_category.notes: Amazon SP-API is a REST-based API for Amazon sellers and vendors to programmatically access their e-commerce data.

one_liner.value: REST API that enables Amazon sellers and vendors to programmatically access orders, shipments, payments, catalog, inventory, listings, and other selling data.

methods.notes: Primary auth is OAuth 2.0 via Login with Amazon (LWA). Uses grant_type=refresh_token for seller-authorized operations and grant_type=client_credentials for grantless operations. Also supports self-authorization for private apps.

access.notes: To register as a developer, you must have a Professional selling account ($39.99/month) and be the primary account user. Amazon evaluates your developer profile after submission. The planned SP-API usage fees ($1,400/year) were cancelled in May 2026. Public developers must also list their app in the Appstore.

summary.value: Comprehensive REST API with 30+ sub-APIs covering Catalog Items, Orders, FBA (Inventory, Inbound, Outbound), Feeds, Reports, Notifications, Product Pricing, Listings Items, Merchant Fulfillment, Finances, Sales, and more. Also includes webhooks via Notifications API. Official SDKs available for Java, Python, C#, JavaScript, PHP.

agent_interface.notes: Amazon published an official MCP server as part of the selling-partner-api-samples repo. The npm package @amazon-sp-api-release/sp-api-dev-mcp bundles two MCP servers: sp-api-dev-assistant-mcp-server (code generation, API reference, optimization) and sp-api-workflow-mcp-server (multi-step workflow builder). Also several community MCP servers exist.

rationale.value: Amazon SP-API is fully buildable as an agent toolkit today. It has a well-documented public REST API with broad coverage, official SDKs in multiple languages, and Amazon itself has already published an official MCP server. The main access requirement is a Professional selling account ($39.99/month) which is a standard business prerequisite rather than a technical blocker. The planned API usage fees were cancelled in May 2026, removing a significant potential barrier. An agent toolkit can integrate using OAuth 2.0 (LWA) with client_id, client_secret, and refresh_token.

s1.quote: Selling Partner API (SP-API) is a REST-based API that helps Amazon sellers and vendors programmatically access their data for orders, shipments, payments, and much more.
s1.title: Selling Partner API Documentation

s2.quote: With Selling Partner API (SP-API), you can build automated solutions for your own sales in the Amazon store. Or you can create and list apps for other Amazon sellers in the Selling Partner Appstore.
s2.title: Selling Partner API (SP-API) | Sell on Amazon

s3.quote: A Login with Amazon (LWA) access token authorizes your application to take certain actions on behalf of a selling partner. To request an LWA access token, make a secure HTTP POST to the LWA authentication server (https://api.amazon.com/auth/o2/token)
s3.title: Connect to the SP-API

s4.quote: You must have a Professional selling account and be the primary account user to create an SP-API developer profile.
s4.title: Selling Partner API (SP-API) | Sell on Amazon

s5.quote: Note that in Seller Central, you must have a professional selling account to develop SP-API private seller applications. Individual accounts are not eligible.
s5.title: SP-API Registration Overview

s6.quote: Authorization method: OAuth 2.0 through Login with Amazon (LWA).
s6.title: Authorize Applications

s7.quote: May 12, 2026: Amazon cancels the SP-API usage and annual fees - the $1,400 subscription and $0.40 per 1,000 GET-call overage are both withdrawn.
s7.title: Amazon SP-API fees cancelled - full chronology (2025-2026)

s8.quote: Prebuilt SDKs are maintained by Amazon and can be added to your application with fewer lines of code. SP-API supports prebuilt SDKs for C#, Java, JavaScript, PHP, and Python.
s8.title: SP-API SDKs

s9.quote: APIs: Catalog Items, Orders, FBA Inventory, Feeds, Reports, Notifications, Product Pricing, Listings Items, Merchant Fulfillment, Finances, Sales, Fulfillment Inbound, Fulfillment Outbound, and many more.
s9.title: Selling Partner API - API Directory

s10.quote: Local MCP for SP-API brings this to the SP-API ecosystem as a consolidated npm package bundling MCP servers for Amazon Selling Partner API development. npm:@amazon-sp-api-release/sp-api-dev-mcp
s10.title: Now Available: Local MCP for SP-API

s11.quote: This repository contains sample code in various programming languages for use cases supported by the Amazon Selling Partner API (SP-API).
s11.title: amzn/selling-partner-api-samples

s12.quote: This guide is best suited for public developers getting started with Amazon SP-API.
s12.title: Amazon Selling Partner API (SP-API) - Getting Started Guide

limitations: Could not find explicit mention of free trial availability for developers without a Professional selling account.
The SP-API has complex IAM role-based signing (AWS Signature V4) in addition to LWA tokens which adds integration complexity.
